My Story PhD Research
Scroll to explore
My Origin
01

A Journey
from Nepal

🇳🇵

I was born and raised in a small town in Nepal — a place where big dreams had to travel far to find their stage. From an early age, I was drawn to understanding how things work: the mechanics of systems, the logic of technology, and the patterns hidden in data.

That curiosity carried me to India, where I studied Information Technology at GVP College of Engineering, graduating First Class with Distinction. I built drones from scratch, designed IoT smart systems, and contributed to a government aerospace project — all while pursuing what would become an award-winning undergraduate research project on using image processing to detect diabetic retinopathy.

Then came the leap to the United Kingdom. The University of Leicester gave me the intellectual home I had always wanted. I graduated MSc Management with Distinction, won the Dean's Award as Best Overall MSc Student, and discovered that the intersection of AI, operations, and human systems was where I was meant to build.

Today, I stand at the threshold of a PhD — not just as an academic milestone, but as the next chapter in a story that started in a small town and now spans three countries and multiple industries.

03

Engineering Projects

From building drones to detecting eye disease with code — hands-on engineering from day one.

Government Drone Project · 2018–2019
Drone Design & System Integration
Contributed to a government-commissioned drone project — system design, hardware integration, firmware setup, and field testing. Hands-on with autopilot controllers, ESCs, motors, and real-time telemetry systems.
ArduPilotEmbedded SystemsField TestingSystem Integration
BTech Final Year · 2019 — Best Project Award
Diabetic Retinopathy Detection via RGB Filtering
Award-winning undergraduate project: built an image processing pipeline using RGB channel filtering to detect early signs of diabetic retinopathy in retinal fundus images — demonstrating how computational thinking solves real healthcare problems.
Image ProcessingRGB FilteringMedical AIPython
IoT · Smart Systems · 2018 & 2024–Present
IoT Smart Home Automation & Golf Simulator CPS
Built sensor-driven smart home automation systems using embedded controllers, relays, and real-time monitoring during BTech. Later applied CPS thinking professionally at Swing Lounge & One Golf — managing IoT-embedded golf simulators, diagnosing live system faults, and optimising sensor performance across deployed sites. This dual experience of building and operating Cyber-Physical Systems forms a core pillar of my PhD research foundation.
IoT SensorsCPSSmart AutomationRemote MonitoringReal-Time Diagnostics
04

Professional Journey

Feb 2024 — Present
Hyderabad, India
Operations & Experience Manager
Swing Lounge & One Golf
Managing IoT-embedded golf simulator systems in a live Cyber-Physical Systems environment. Real-time data analysis, remote diagnostics, and system performance optimisation across multiple deployed sites. Improved user experience through proactive sensor optimisation and issue resolution.
Mar 2024 — Mar 2026
United Kingdom
Warehouse Operative & Reserve Coordinator
Tesco Frozen
Real-time workforce allocation and operational planning in a high-volume logistics environment. Working directly with demand forecasting data to optimise staffing and inventory. This role provided the lived operational frustrations that now drive my PhD research on AI-integrated workforce systems.
Apr 2021 — Sept 2022
India
Account Manager
NxtWave Disruptive Technologies
Managed data-driven marketing campaigns generating 1,000+ qualified leads contributing to ₹2 Crore in revenue. First-hand experience of the demand-operations disconnect: marketing generates leads but operations never adapts in time — the exact gap my PhD research aims to close.
Jun 2019 — Mar 2021
India
Business Development Executive / Zonal Head
iB Cricket
Contributed to development of a VR-based interactive cricket platform — gaining deep experience in immersive human-computer interaction. Delivered 50+ large-scale events significantly increasing regional revenue. Managed VR/AR user experiences combining physical and digital environments.
05

PhD Research

"AI-Driven Integration of Customer Acquisition and Workforce Optimisation: A Cross-Industry Study"

Most organisations operate with a fundamental disconnect — marketing generates demand while operations manages resources in complete isolation. The result is chronic understaffing, overstaffing, reactive decisions, and wasted potential.

Having lived this problem on both sides — as a marketer at NxtWave watching demand surge without operational response, and as a warehouse coordinator at Tesco unable to adapt to demand spikes in time — I am uniquely positioned to solve it.

My research aims to build a unified AI framework that connects customer demand signals directly to operational workforce decisions in real time, validated across EdTech, logistics, and digital platforms.

Request Research Statement →
01
Demand Prediction Model

ML system trained on marketing and customer data to forecast future operational demand in real time.

02
Workforce Optimisation

Optimisation algorithms recommending staffing levels, scheduling, and task allocation based on predicted demand.

03
Integrated AI Decision Framework

Unified intelligent dashboard with human-in-the-loop decision support connecting marketing to operations.

04
Cross-Industry Validation

Scenario-based testing across EdTech, logistics, and digital platforms to ensure generalisability.
